description: The chart uses two different y-axis scales for the three bar groups: "Furnished" and "Semi-furnished" bars are plotted on the left axis (starting at €0), while the "Unfurnished" bars use a right axis that starts at €2.0M and has a compressed range. This makes the "Unfurnished" bars appear visually comparable in height to the others, falsely suggesting the price gap between furnishing statuses is much smaller than it actually is — a reader glancing at bar heights alone would severely underestimate how much cheaper unfurnished homes are.
